ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "estimated_yearly_income" INTEGER /* Estimated yearly income of the customer */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the customer */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contracts" (
  "contract_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contract */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the contract */,
  "contract_type" TEXT /* Type of contract (e.g., individual, family) */,
  "contract_start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the contract */,
  "contract_end_date" TEXT /* End date of the contract */,
  "contract_status" TEXT /* Status of the contract (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "renewal_date" TEXT /* Date when the contract was renewed */,
  "contract_value" INTEGER /* Value of the contract */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contract_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contracts_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_value_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who belongs to the segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment (e.g., high value, low risk) */,
  "predicted_lifetime_value" REAL /* Predicted lifetime value of the customer */,
  "churn_risk" REAL /* Churn risk score of the customer */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_value_segments_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "churn_scores" (
  "churn_score_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each churn score */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the churn score */,
  "churn_score" REAL /* Churn score of the customer */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the churn score was predicted */,
  "model_used" TEXT /* Model used to predict the churn score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("churn_score_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_churn_scores_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "call_activity" (
  "call_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each call */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the call */,
  "call_date" TEXT /* Date when the call was made */,
  "call_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the call in minutes */,
  "call_type" TEXT /* Type of call (e.g., incoming, outgoing) */,
  "call_result" TEXT /* Result of the call (e.g., answered, missed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("call_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_call_activity_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiment_ratings" (
  "sentiment_rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment rating */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the sentiment rating */,
  "sentiment_rating" INTEGER /* Sentiment rating of the customer (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the sentiment rating was given */,
  "rating_source" TEXT /* Source of the sentiment rating (e.g., survey, review) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiment_ratings_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_type" TEXT /* Type of product (e.g., phone, internet) */,
  "product_description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "product_price" INTEGER /* Price of the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_products" (
  "customer_product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer product */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the product */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product owned by the customer */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date when the product was purchased */,
  "purchase_price" INTEGER /* Price at which the product was purchased */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_products_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_products_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "web_activity" (
  "web_activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each web activity */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who performed the web activity */,
  "web_activity_date" TEXT /* Date when the web activity was performed */,
  "web_activity_type" TEXT /* Type of web activity (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "web_activity_url" TEXT /* URL of the web activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("web_activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_web_activity_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "available_401k" (
  "available_401k_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each available 401k */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the available 401k */,
  "available_401k" INTEGER /* Available 401k amount */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the available 401k was last updated */,
  "contribution_rate" REAL /* Contribution rate to the 401k */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("available_401k_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_available_401k_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the prediction */,
  "prediction" INTEGER /* Prediction made by the decision tree model */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model used to make the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "target" (
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each target */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the target */,
  "target" INTEGER /* Target value */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date when the target was last updated */,
  "target_description" TEXT /* Description of the target */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("target_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_target_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "social_media" (
  "social_media_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who owns the social media record */,
  "social_media_platform" TEXT /* Social media platform (e.g., Twitter, Facebook) */,
  "social_media_handle" TEXT /* Social media handle of the customer */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("social_media_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_social_media_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_trends" (
  "market_trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market trend */,
  "trend_name" TEXT /* Name of the market trend */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Description of the market trend */,
  "trend_date" TEXT /* Date when the market trend was observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("market_trend_id")
)
